theory.intro: To graph a linear relationship, draw its straight line on the Cartesian plane. Build a short table of values from the equation, plot each $(x,y)$ column as a point, then rule one straight line through the points and extend it past the outermost points.
facts: A relationship written as $y = mx + c$ always graphs as a straight line.
Each column of the table of values is a coordinate $(x,y)$ to plot.
Two points fix a line, but plotting three or more catches a slip: if one point is off the line through the others, recheck that column.
Rule the line right across the grid and add an arrow on each end to show it continues.
On the same axes, a line with a bigger number in front of $x$ is steeper; a positive $x$-coefficient rises left to right, a negative one falls.

foundation.question_text: Graph the line $y = x + 2$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$.
foundation.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&2&3&4&5\end{array}$
$(0,2),(1,3),(2,4),(3,5)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=6,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.7:4.7,samples=2] {x+2};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,2) (1,3) (2,4) (3,5)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

foundation.question_text: Graph the line $y = 2x + 1$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$.
foundation.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&1&3&5&7\end{array}$
$(0,1),(1,3),(2,5),(3,7)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=9,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,1,3,5,7,9},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.7:3.9,samples=2] {2*x+1};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,1) (1,3) (2,5) (3,7)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

foundation.question_text: Graph the line $y = 3x - 1$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$.
foundation.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&-1&2&5&8\end{array}$
$(0,-1),(1,2),(2,5),(3,8)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-2,ymax=9,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-2,0,2,4,6,8},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.3:3.3,samples=2] {3*x-1};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,-1) (1,2) (2,5) (3,8)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

foundation.question_text: Graph the line $y = 4 - x$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$.
foundation.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&4&3&2&1\end{array}$
$(0,4),(1,3),(2,2),(3,1)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=5,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.7:4.7,samples=2] {-x+4};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,4) (1,3) (2,2) (3,1)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

foundation.question_text: Graph the line $y = 2x$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$.
foundation.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&0&2&4&6\end{array}$
$(0,0),(1,2),(2,4),(3,6)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=7,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,1,3,5,7},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.5:3.5,samples=2] {2*x};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,0) (1,2) (2,4) (3,6)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

development.question_text: Graph the line $y = x - 1$ using a table of values with $x = -2, -1, 0, 1, 2$.
development.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|ccccc}x&-2&-1&0&1&2\\\hline y&-3&-2&-1&0&1\end{array}$
$(-2,-3),(-1,-2),(0,-1),(1,0),(2,1)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-3,xmax=3,ymin=-4,ymax=2,xtick={-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3},ytick={-4,-3,-2,-1,0,1,2},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-2.7:2.7,samples=2] {x-1};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(-2,-3) (-1,-2) (0,-1) (1,0) (2,1)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

development.question_text: Graph the line $y = -2x - 1$ using a table of values with $x = -2, -1, 0, 1, 2$.
development.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|ccccc}x&-2&-1&0&1&2\\\hline y&3&1&-1&-3&-5\end{array}$
$(-2,3),(-1,1),(0,-1),(1,-3),(2,-5)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-3,xmax=3,ymin=-6,ymax=4,xtick={-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3},ytick={-6,-4,-2,0,2,4},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-2.3:2.3,samples=2] {-2*x-1};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(-2,3) (-1,1) (0,-1) (1,-3) (2,-5)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

development.question_text: Graph the line $y = 3 - x$ using a table of values with $x = -2, -1, 0, 1, 2$.
development.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|ccccc}x&-2&-1&0&1&2\\\hline y&5&4&3&2&1\end{array}$
$(-2,5),(-1,4),(0,3),(1,2),(2,1)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-3,xmax=4,ymin=-1,ymax=6,xtick={-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3,4},ytick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-2.7:3.5,samples=2] {-x+3};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(-2,5) (-1,4) (0,3) (1,2) (2,1)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

development.question_text: Graph the line $y = \frac{1}{2}x + 1$ using a table of values with $x = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4$.
development.solution_text:
$\begin{array}{c|ccccc}x&0&1&2&3&4\\\hline y&1&1.5&2&2.5&3\end{array}$
$(0,1),(1,1.5),(2,2),(3,2.5),(4,3)$
The half-unit points $(1,1.5)$ and $(3,2.5)$ sit midway between grid lines.
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=4,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,0,1,2,3,4},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.7:4.7,samples=2] {0.5*x+1};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,1) (1,1.5) (2,2) (3,2.5) (4,3)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-5,ymax=5,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-4,-2,0,2,4},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.5:4,samples=2] {2*x-3};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,-3) (1,-1) (2,1) (3,3)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The line is graphed above. Which equation does it show: $y = 2x - 3$, $y = x - 3$, or $y = 2x + 3$?

mastery.question_text: Graph $y = x + 1$ and $y = 2x + 1$ on the same axes using $x = 0, 1, 2, 3$. State one way the lines are the same and one way they are different.
mastery.solution_text:
$y=x+1:\quad (0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)$
$y=2x+1:\quad (0,1),(1,3),(2,5),(3,7)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6.4cm,height=5.8cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=5,ymin=-1,ymax=8,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},ytick={-1,1,3,5,7},minor y tick num=1,grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,blue,<->,domain=-0.7:4.7,samples=2] {x+1};\addplot[only marks,blue,mark=*,mark size=1.6pt] coordinates {(0,1) (1,2) (2,3) (3,4)};\addplot[thick,red,dashed,<->,domain=-0.7:3.5,samples=2] {2*x+1};\addplot[only marks,red,mark=*,mark size=1.6pt] coordinates {(0,1) (1,3) (2,5) (3,7)};\node[red] at (axis cs:1.5,6.6) {$y=2x+1$};\node[blue] at (axis cs:4.1,3.3) {$y=x+1$};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Both lines cross the $y$-axis at $(0,1)$. They differ in steepness: $y=2x+1$ rises $2$ for each $1$ across, so it is steeper than $y=x+1$, which rises $1$.

mastery.question_text: Graph $y = x + 2$ and $y = -x + 2$ on the same axes using $x = -2, 0, 2$. State one way the lines are the same and one way they are different.
mastery.solution_text:
$y=x+2:\quad (-2,0),(0,2),(2,4)$
$y=-x+2:\quad (-2,4),(0,2),(2,0)$
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6.4cm,height=5.8cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-3,xmax=3,ymin=-1,ymax=5,xtick={-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3},ytick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,blue,<->,domain=-2.7:2.7,samples=2] {x+2};\addplot[only marks,blue,mark=*,mark size=1.6pt] coordinates {(-2,0) (0,2) (2,4)};\addplot[thick,red,dashed,<->,domain=-2.7:2.7,samples=2] {-x+2};\addplot[only marks,red,mark=*,mark size=1.6pt] coordinates {(-2,4) (0,2) (2,0)};\node[blue] at (axis cs:1.4,4.5) {$y=x+2$};\node[red] at (axis cs:-1.5,4.5) {$y=-x+2$};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Both lines cross the $y$-axis at $(0,2)$. They differ in direction: $y=x+2$ rises from left to right, while $y=-x+2$ falls.
